title: First Principles Biology for NEET

concepts.title: What Is “Life”? – Cells as the Basic Unit
concepts.brief: Defining life (organization, metabolism, homeostasis, reproduction, response, evolution); cell theory; prokaryotes vs eukaryotes. Subtopics: levels of biological organization, basic cell architecture, why compartmentalization matters. NCERT: Class 11 'The Living World', 'Biological Classification', 'Cell: The Unit of Life'. NEET angle: classification questions, cell structure diagrams, 'not a characteristic of living organisms' traps.

concepts.title: The Chemistry of Life – Biomolecules and Enzymes
concepts.brief: Life is chemistry in water: key elements, bonds, energy. Macromolecules as functional polymers; enzymes as catalysts lowering activation energy. Subtopics: carbohydrates/proteins/lipids/nucleic acids structure->function, enzyme kinetics (active site, specificity, inhibition), ATP as energy currency. NCERT: Class 11 'Biomolecules', 'Cell: The Unit of Life' (organelles), 'Plant Physiology' (energy). NEET angle: biomolecule identification, enzyme function, 'which is not a protein' traps.

concepts.title: Energy and Matter – Thermodynamics for Biologists
concepts.brief: Energy cannot be created/destroyed; organisms transform it. Exergonic vs endergonic reactions, coupling via ATP; matter cycles, energy flows. Subtopics: laws of thermodynamics in biological context, free energy/entropy (conceptual), how cells export entropy to maintain order. NCERT: Class 11 'Plant Physiology' (photosynthesis, respiration), 'Body Fluids and Circulation', 'Ecology' (energy flow). NEET angle: photosynthesis vs respiration comparisons, energy pyramid questions.

concepts.title: The Genetic Code – DNA, RNA, and the Central Dogma
concepts.brief: Genetic information stored, copied, expressed; DNA->RNA->Protein central flow; mutations as information changes. Subtopics: DNA/RNA structure, semi-conservative replication, transcription/translation, genetic code universality/degeneracy. NCERT: Class 12 'Molecular Basis of Inheritance'. NEET angle: enzymes in replication/transcription, codon questions, mutation effects.

concepts.title: Inheritance – How Traits Pass Across Generations
concepts.brief: Genes as heredity units, alleles create variation; segregation/independent assortment generate diversity; probability underlies inheritance. Subtopics: Mendel's laws, monohybrid/dihybrid crosses, dominance/co-dominance/incomplete dominance, chromosomal theory, linkage/recombination (conceptual). NCERT: Class 12 'Principles of Inheritance and Variation'. NEET angle: pedigree analysis, cross problems, unexpected ratios.

concepts.title: From Genotype to Phenotype – Gene Expression and Regulation
concepts.brief: Genes aren't always 'on'; regulation is key; environment/development shape phenotype. Subtopics: lac operon at NEET level, differential gene expression in development, examples (blood groups, sickle-cell anemia, thalassemia). NCERT: Class 12 'Molecular Basis of Inheritance', 'Principles of Inheritance and Variation'. NEET angle: regulation questions, human genetic disorders.

concepts.title: Evolution – Change in Populations Over Time
concepts.brief: Evolution = change in allele frequencies; variation + selection + time -> adaptation; evidence from independent lines. Subtopics: origin of life basics, Darwinism and modern synthesis, Hardy-Weinberg equilibrium (concept + simple numericals), speciation/isolation mechanisms, human evolution (high level). NCERT: Class 12 'Evolution'. NEET angle: Hardy-Weinberg problems, evidences of evolution, timeline questions.

concepts.title: Plant Body Plan – From Cells to Organ Systems
concepts.brief: Plant structure optimized for light capture, gas exchange, support; tissues->organs->whole plant function. Subtopics: meristematic/permanent tissues, anatomy of root/stem/leaf, how structure supports transport and photosynthesis. NCERT: Class 11 'Structural Organisation in Plants', 'Anatomy of Flowering Plants'. NEET angle: tissue identification, diagram-based questions.

concepts.title: Water and Minerals – Transport in Plants
concepts.brief: Movement driven by gradients + selective barriers + energy input; water potential as unifying concept. Subtopics: diffusion/osmosis/plasmolysis, ascent of sap (cohesion-tension theory), transpiration and stomatal mechanism, mineral uptake and transport. NCERT: Class 11 'Transport in Plants', 'Mineral Nutrition'. NEET angle: water potential problems, transpiration factors, deficiency symptoms.

concepts.title: Energy Capture and Release – Photosynthesis and Respiration
concepts.brief: Photosynthesis: light->chemical energy; respiration: chemical energy->ATP; coupled pathways. Subtopics: light reaction and Calvin cycle (conceptual flow), C3/C4/CAM strategies and why they exist, glycolysis/Krebs/ETS/oxidative phosphorylation, RQ and metabolic flexibility. NCERT: Class 11 'Photosynthesis in Higher Plants', 'Respiration in Plants'. NEET angle: pathway steps, inhibitors, C3 vs C4 vs CAM, RQ questions.

concepts.title: Growth, Development, and Control – Plant Hormones and Responses
concepts.brief: Growth is regulated, not random; hormones coordinate responses; plants integrate internal signals and environment. Subtopics: auxins/gibberellins/cytokinins/ABA/ethylene, photoperiodism/vernalization, tropisms and nastic movements (conceptual). NCERT: Class 11 'Plant Growth and Development'. NEET angle: hormone functions, applications (fruit ripening, bolting), response questions.

concepts.title: The Human Body – Organization and Homeostasis
concepts.brief: Multicellular organization: cells->tissues->organs->systems; homeostasis via feedback loops as central theme. Subtopics: tissue types overview (epithelial, connective, muscular, nervous), negative/positive feedback with examples, integration of systems. NCERT: Class 11 'Structural Organisation in Animals', 'Neural Control', 'Chemical Coordination'. NEET angle: tissue identification, feedback examples.

concepts.title: Fuel and Building Blocks – Digestion and Absorption
concepts.brief: Food broken down to absorbable units; enzymatic hydrolysis + surface area optimization. Subtopics: alimentary canal and accessory glands, digestive enzymes (site, substrate, product), absorption/assimilation basics, disorders (jaundice, ulcers, malabsorption). NCERT: Class 11 'Digestion and Absorption'. NEET angle: enzyme-substrate matching, site of action, disorder questions.

concepts.title: Gas Exchange – Breathing and Transport of Gases
concepts.brief: Diffusion across thin moist surfaces driven by partial pressure gradients; blood as transport medium for O2/CO2. Subtopics: human respiratory system anatomy, mechanism of breathing, lung volumes/capacities, transport of O2 (Hb saturation) and CO2 (bicarbonate, Hb), disorders (asthma, emphysema, occupational diseases). NCERT: Class 11 'Breathing and Exchange of Gases'. NEET angle: partial pressure concepts, lung volume definitions.

concepts.title: Internal Transport – Body Fluids and Circulation
concepts.brief: Closed circulatory system as pressure-driven distribution network; heart as dual pump; blood as transport + defense fluid. Subtopics: blood composition/functions, heart anatomy/cardiac cycle/ECG basics, double circulation, regulation of cardiac activity, disorders (hypertension, coronary artery disease, heart failure). NCERT: Class 11 'Body Fluids and Circulation'. NEET angle: ECG interpretation basics, blood groups, circulation pathway questions.

concepts.title: Waste Management – Excretory Products and Elimination
concepts.brief: Nitrogenous waste removed while conserving water/ions; kidney as selective filter+reabsorber+secretor. Subtopics: types of nitrogenous wastes, human excretory system/nephron structure, urine formation (filtration/reabsorption/secretion), osmoregulation (ADH, RAAS), disorders (kidney failure, dialysis, kidney stones). NCERT: Class 11 'Excretory Products and Their Elimination'. NEET angle: nephron segment functions, hormone actions, clinical scenarios.

concepts.title: Movement and Support – Locomotion and the Musculoskeletal System
concepts.brief: Movement requires force generation (muscles) and leverage (bones/joints); sliding filament theory as core mechanism. Subtopics: muscle types and skeletal muscle structure, sarcomere and sliding filament theory, joints and common disorders (arthritis, osteoporosis). NCERT: Class 11 'Locomotion and Movement'. NEET angle: muscle contraction steps, joint types, disorder-feature matching.

concepts.title: Control and Coordination – Neural and Chemical Systems
concepts.brief: Rapid control via nerves, slower/widespread control via hormones; integration of nervous and endocrine systems. Subtopics: neuron structure, nerve impulse generation/conduction, synapse and neurotransmission, human brain major parts/functions, eye and ear basic structure, endocrine glands/major hormones/feedback control, disorders (hypo/hyper thyroid, diabetes, Addison's). NCERT: Class 11 'Neural Control and Coordination', 'Chemical Coordination and Integration'. NEET angle: brain part functions, hormone-gland-target matching, reflex arc, sensory organ questions.

concepts.title: Why Reproduce? – Strategies Across Life
concepts.brief: Reproduction ensures continuity of species; trade-offs between asexual and sexual modes; sexual reproduction increases variation. Subtopics: asexual reproduction (binary fission, budding, fragmentation, spores, vegetative propagation), sexual reproduction basic steps (gametogenesis->fertilization->zygote->embryo), life spans and reproductive strategies. NCERT: Class 12 'Reproduction in Organisms'. NEET angle: examples of organisms and modes, advantages/disadvantages.

concepts.title: Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants
concepts.brief: Flowers are reproductive organs optimized for pollination and seed formation; double fertilization as unique plant feature. Subtopics: flower structure, microsporogenesis/megasporogenesis, pollination types/agents, fertilization (pollen-pistil interaction, double fertilization), post-fertilization (seed/fruit formation, apomixis, polyembryony). NCERT: Class 12 'Sexual Reproduction in Flowering Plants'. NEET angle: diagram-based questions, sequence of events, special phenomena.

concepts.title: Human Reproduction and Reproductive Health
concepts.brief: Human reproduction tightly regulated by hormones and feedback; reproductive health impacts individuals and populations. Subtopics: male/female reproductive systems, gametogenesis (spermatogenesis, oogenesis), menstrual cycle phases and hormonal control, fertilization/implantation/pregnancy/parturition/lactation, reproductive health (contraception, STDs, ARTs like IVF, population issues). NCERT: Class 12 'Human Reproduction', 'Reproductive Health'. NEET angle: hormone roles, cycle phases, contraceptive methods, ART basics.

concepts.title: Health and Disease – Biology in Medicine
concepts.brief: Disease is disruption of normal biological function; immunity is the body's defense system, vaccines exploit immune memory. Subtopics: common pathogens (bacteria, viruses, protozoa, fungi - NEET examples), immunity (innate vs acquired, antibodies, vaccination), cancer/AIDS/allergies (basic mechanisms), lifestyle and non-communicable diseases. NCERT: Class 12 'Human Health and Disease'. NEET angle: pathogen-disease matching, immunity concepts, vaccine questions.

concepts.title: Microbes in Human Welfare
concepts.brief: Microbes are tools: they transform materials and produce useful compounds. Subtopics: microbes in household products (curd, bread), industrial products (antibiotics, enzymes, beverages), sewage treatment and biogas, microbes in biocontrol and biofertilizers. NCERT: Class 12 'Microbes in Human Welfare'. NEET angle: microbe-product matching, process steps, applications.

concepts.title: Improving Food Production – Plant and Animal Breeding
concepts.brief: Selective breeding and biotechnology enhance yield and quality; genetic variation is the raw material for improvement. Subtopics: plant breeding steps, high-yielding varieties, single cell proteins, biofortification, animal breeding (inbreeding, outbreeding, MOET), tissue culture and plant propagation. NCERT: Class 12 'Strategies for Enhancement in Food Production'. NEET angle: breeding methods, improved varieties, tissue culture applications.

concepts.title: Biotechnology – Principles and Applications
concepts.brief: Biotechnology = using living systems to make products; recombinant DNA technology as core toolset. Subtopics: tools (restriction enzymes, vectors, host organisms), processes (cloning, PCR, gel electrophoresis - conceptual), applications (insulin, Bt crops, gene therapy, transgenic animals, DNA fingerprinting), biosafety/ethical issues. NCERT: Class 12 'Biotechnology: Principles and Processes', 'Biotechnology and Its Applications'. NEET angle: tool-function matching, rDNA technology steps, application examples.

concepts.title: Organisms and Populations
concepts.brief: Organisms shaped by environment; populations change over time; interactions drive population dynamics. Subtopics: habitat/niche/adaptations, population attributes (density, birth/death rates, age pyramids), population interactions (competition, predation, mutualism, etc). NCERT: Class 12 'Organisms and Populations'. NEET angle: adaptation examples, interaction types, population growth curves.

concepts.title: Ecosystems – Structure and Function
concepts.brief: Ecosystems are units where energy flows and matter cycles; trophic structure determines energy distribution. Subtopics: ecosystem components (biotic/abiotic), productivity (GPP, NPP), food chains/webs/ecological pyramids, ecological succession, nutrient cycles (carbon, phosphorus - conceptual). NCERT: Class 12 'Ecosystem'. NEET angle: pyramid types, productivity calculations, succession stages.

concepts.title: Biodiversity, Conservation, and Environmental Issues
concepts.brief: Biodiversity supports ecosystem stability and human welfare; human activities can degrade or restore ecosystems. Subtopics: levels/patterns of biodiversity, importance and threats, conservation (in-situ, ex-situ), environmental issues (pollution, global warming, ozone depletion, deforestation, case studies like eutrophication, Chipko). NCERT: Class 12 'Biodiversity and Conservation', 'Environmental Issues'. NEET angle: conservation strategies, pollution types, environmental movements, treaties (basic).

images.prompt: A clean, modern editorial cover illustration for a first-principles biology study book: a single luminous cell (visible nucleus, mitochondria, simple organelles rendered as soft geometric shapes) opening outward into a faint tree-of-life / branching network that fades into the page edges, suggesting 'one cell, one idea, branching into all of biology.' Flat vector style, generous white negative space, deep navy/charcoal linework, one muted teal accent and one soft coral accent used sparingly as highlight dots along the branches, no text or labels baked into the image, calm and academic rather than busy or cartoonish. Target size ~960x1280px, 3:4 portrait; compose for a book-cover panel -- keep the composition centered with safe margins on all sides since it is letterboxed into a 3:4 box.
images.alt: Cover artwork for First Principles Biology for NEET
images.caption: One cell, one idea at a time -- the whole of NEET biology, built from first principles.

notes: Requested look: deep navy/charcoal headings and strong lines on a clean white background (no existing theme id matched a clean-white-background look, so design a bespoke theme rather than reusing a dark-background default). Accents: one calm color for 'concept' elements (teal/muted green) and one warm color for 'NEET focus' elements (soft orange/coral), used sparingly (section headers, key concept boxes, 'NEET trap'/'Exam tip' callouts, diagram highlights) so the page doesn't read as a rainbow -- green box = core concept, orange box = NEET exam focus. Typography: at most two typefaces -- a clean readable serif or humanist sans for body text, a matching bold/sans for headings, with a consistent size hierarchy (chapter title large bold, section headings medium, subheadings smaller/italic-or-small-caps), generous line spacing (1.2-1.35), comfortable margins for annotation. Emotional core: 'clarity over clutter', 'understanding over memorization', calm/structured/trustworthy -- avoid exam-stress visuals (red alarms, 'CRACK NEET IN 30 DAYS!' vibes) and avoid a childish/cartoonish school-textbook feel; aim for a clean modern academic look between a good university intro text and a high-quality coaching module. Book structure requested by the user: an introductory 'How to Use This Book' section (who it's for, how it differs from NCERT/question banks, a 4-step study loop -- light NCERT read, this book's concept chapter, re-read NCERT with understanding, solve MCQs/PYQs -- and how concept maps/'NEET traps'/'Think & Answer' sections work) -- fold this into the index.html cover/intro rather than a separate concept page. The book is organized into 7 parts (Foundations; Information Flow/Genetics; Plant Structure & Function; Human Structure & Function; Reproduction & Evolution/Continuity; Applications: Health/Food/Biotechnology; Ecology) -- honor this grouping as section dividers in the table of contents even though concepts are stored as one flat ordered list. Every chapter must explicitly map back to its NCERT Class 11/12 chapter(s) and call out a NEET exam angle (traps, question patterns) -- treat 'NCERT mapping' and 'NEET angle' as required per-page content, not optional flavor; each concept's brief below already carries its NCERT mapping and NEET angle. Fold the user's requested appendices (a hormones/glands/targets quick table, an enzymes/sites/substrates table, a diseases/causative-agents table, and a 'common NEET traps' list) into cheatsheet.html rather than as separate concept pages. Use illustrations/diagrams (inline SVG per write-book's Images & diagrams section) where profoundly useful -- e.g. cell structure, central dogma flow, Hardy-Weinberg/allele frequency, C3/C4/CAM comparison, nephron structure, sliding filament theory, reflex arc, ecological pyramids -- concept-first explanations, not memorization tables, are the book's whole premise.
